table.name: send_orders
table.description: Order-level master table for Ping (quick commerce delivery) orders. One row per order_nr. Contains order metadata, delivery timestamps, logistics distances, customer segmentation flags, and payment details.

refresh.strategy: CREATE OR REPLACE — full lifetime rebuild every 4 hours

columns.name: order_nr
columns.description: Unique order identifier. Primary grain of the table.

columns.name: customer_code
columns.description: Unique customer identifier (UUID).

columns.name: day
columns.description: Order creation date in GST (UTC+4). Partition column.

columns.name: hour
columns.description: Hour of order creation in GST (0–23).

columns.name: mealtime
columns.description: Mealtime bucket derived from order hour in GST.

columns.name: is_testing
columns.description: 0 = real order, 1 = test order. Table only contains 0 (test orders filtered at source).

columns.name: order_status
columns.description: Lowercased order status.

columns.name: payment_method
columns.description: Cleaned payment method label.

columns.name: order_payment_amount
columns.description: Order value / fee paid by customer. Use only for delivered orders for GMV/AOV calculations.

columns.name: package_type
columns.description: Type/size of the package for the order.

columns.name: city
columns.description: City where the order was fulfilled, derived from fleet assignment.
columns.source: noondwh.scfood_foodlogistics.city via task → fleet → city join

columns.name: created_at_gst
columns.description: Order creation timestamp in GST (UTC+4).

columns.name: assigned_at_gst
columns.description: Rider assignment timestamp in GST. NULL if never assigned.

columns.name: picked_up_at_gst
columns.description: Order pickup timestamp in GST. NULL if never picked up.

columns.name: delivered_at_gst
columns.description: Order delivery timestamp in GST. NULL if not delivered.

columns.name: time_to_assignment_min
columns.description: Minutes from order creation to rider assignment. NULL for non-delivered orders.

columns.name: time_to_pickup_min
columns.description: Minutes from rider assignment to order pickup. NULL for non-delivered orders.

columns.name: time_to_delivery_min
columns.description: Minutes from pickup to delivery (last mile time). NULL for non-delivered orders.

columns.name: o2d_minutes
columns.description: Order-to-door: total minutes from order creation to delivery. Key SLA metric. NULL for non-delivered orders.

columns.name: lm_km
columns.description: Last mile distance in km — rider to customer. NULL if no logistics record exists.

columns.name: fm_km
columns.description: First mile distance in km — store/dark store to rider pickup point. NULL if no logistics record exists.

columns.name: first_order_date
columns.description: Date of the customer's first ever Ping order (lifetime, across all history).

columns.name: is_new_customer
columns.description: TRUE if this order is the customer's first ever Ping order (day = first_order_date).

columns.name: is_repeat_customer
columns.description: TRUE if the customer has placed a Ping order before this one (day > first_order_date).

columns.name: is_n1_customer
columns.description: TRUE if the customer was flagged as N1 (top-tier Noon customer) on that specific order day. Sourced from CRM daily flags, AE country only, matched on both customer_code and day.

columns.name: nationality
columns.description: Customer nationality bucketed into top nationalities. Any nationality outside the top list is grouped as 'Others'.

columns.name: delivery_notes
columns.description: Customer-provided delivery instructions / notes extracted from delivery_instructions JSON.

columns.name: should_save
columns.description: Flag from delivery_instructions JSON indicating whether the address should be saved.

common_filters.exclude_test_orders: is_testing = 0 (already filtered at table level — no additional filter needed)
common_filters.date_range: WHERE day BETWEEN DATE('YYYY-MM-DD') AND DATE('YYYY-MM-DD')

common_metrics.delivered_orders: COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN order_status = 'delivered' THEN order_nr END)
common_metrics.cancelled_orders: COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN order_status = 'cancelled' THEN order_nr END)
common_metrics.failed_orders: COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN order_status = 'failed' THEN order_nr END)
common_metrics.delivery_rate_pct: ROUND(SAFE_DIVIDE(COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN order_status = 'delivered' THEN order_nr END), COUNT(DISTINCT order_nr)) * 100, 1)
common_metrics.gmv: SUM(CASE WHEN order_status = 'delivered' THEN order_payment_amount ELSE 0 END)
common_metrics.aov: SAFE_DIVIDE(SUM(CASE WHEN order_status = 'delivered' THEN order_payment_amount ELSE 0 END), COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN order_status = 'delivered' THEN order_nr END))
common_metrics.avg_o2d_min: AVG(CASE WHEN order_status = 'delivered' THEN o2d_minutes END)
common_metrics.avg_assignment_min: AVG(CASE WHEN order_status = 'delivered' THEN time_to_assignment_min END)
common_metrics.avg_pickup_min: AVG(CASE WHEN order_status = 'delivered' THEN time_to_pickup_min END)
common_metrics.avg_last_mile_min: AVG(CASE WHEN order_status = 'delivered' THEN time_to_delivery_min END)
common_metrics.total_lm_km: SUM(CASE WHEN order_status = 'delivered' THEN lm_km ELSE 0 END)
common_metrics.avg_lm_km: AVG(CASE WHEN order_status = 'delivered' THEN lm_km END)
common_metrics.new_customers: COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN is_new_customer THEN customer_code END)
common_metrics.repeat_customers: COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN is_repeat_customer THEN customer_code END)
common_metrics.n1_customers: COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN is_n1_customer THEN customer_code END)
common_metrics.non_n1_customers: COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN NOT is_n1_customer THEN customer_code END)

example_queries.daily_order_summary: SELECT day, COUNT(DISTINCT order_nr) AS total_orders, COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN order_status = 'delivered' THEN order_nr END) AS delivered_orders FROM `noonbifood.ping.send_orders` GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1
example_queries.daily_aov_gmv: SELECT day, ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN order_status = 'delivered' THEN order_payment_amount END), 2) AS gmv, ROUND(SAFE_DIVIDE(SUM(CASE WHEN order_status = 'delivered' THEN order_payment_amount END), COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN order_status = 'delivered' THEN order_nr END)), 2) AS aov FROM `noonbifood.ping.send_orders` GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1
example_queries.daily_o2d: SELECT day, ROUND(AVG(CASE WHEN order_status = 'delivered' THEN o2d_minutes END), 1) AS avg_o2d FROM `noonbifood.ping.send_orders` GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1
example_queries.n1_vs_non_n1: SELECT day, COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN is_n1_customer THEN customer_code END) AS n1_customers, COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN NOT is_n1_customer THEN customer_code END) AS non_n1_customers FROM `noonbifood.ping.send_orders` WHERE order_status = 'delivered' GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1
example_queries.new_vs_repeat: SELECT day, COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN is_new_customer THEN customer_code END) AS new_customers, COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN is_repeat_customer THEN customer_code END) AS repeat_customers FROM `noonbifood.ping.send_orders` GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1
example_queries.payment_mix: SELECT day, payment_method, COUNT(DISTINCT order_nr) AS orders FROM `noonbifood.ping.send_orders` GROUP BY 1, 2 ORDER BY 1, 2
example_queries.city_day_summary: SELECT day, city, COUNT(DISTINCT order_nr) AS total_orders FROM `noonbifood.ping.send_orders` GROUP BY 1, 2 ORDER BY 1, 2
example_queries.mealtime_split: SELECT day, mealtime, COUNT(DISTINCT order_nr) AS orders FROM `noonbifood.ping.send_orders` GROUP BY 1, 2 ORDER BY 1, 2
example_queries.nationality_split: SELECT day, nationality, COUNT(DISTINCT order_nr) AS orders FROM `noonbifood.ping.send_orders` GROUP BY 1, 2 ORDER BY 1, 2

notes: All timestamps are stored in GST (UTC+4). Raw UTC timestamps are NOT stored — use created_at_gst, assigned_at_gst etc.
Delivery time columns (o2d_minutes, time_to_assignment_min etc.) are NULL for non-delivered orders. Always filter or use CASE WHEN order_status = 'delivered' when aggregating.
lm_km and fm_km are NULL if no logistics record exists for the order — use CASE WHEN or filter accordingly.
is_testing = 0 is already enforced at the table level. No additional filter needed.
is_new_customer and is_repeat_customer are mutually exclusive and derived from lifetime first_order_date — not just the 65-day window.
is_n1_customer is a daily flag — it reflects N1 status on the specific order day, not current status.
nationality is pre-bucketed. 'Others' covers all nationalities outside the top 22 listed.
city can be NULL if the order has no associated task/fleet record in the logistics tables.
Table is partitioned by day — always include a day filter in production queries to minimize bytes scanned.
